reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku (not Tokyo). Tool fetches Akita spots but returns archaeological sites, a lake, a castle, and a museum — none tagged as cherry blossom venues. No seasonal filter applied. The constraint (sakura) and the region (Tohoku as a whole vs single Akita prefecture) are both partially ignored.
missing_data: Cherry blossom spots in Akita/Tohoku: Kakunodate samurai district (famous for sakura), Hirosaki Park (Aomori), Tsuruoka Park (Yamagata), Morioka Shiroishi Park. No seasonal/hanami tagging on any returned spot.
improvement_hint: Add a sakura/hanami keyword filter or tag attractions with seasonal highlights so agents can retrieve flower-viewing spots. Consider broadening Tohoku scope across multiple prefectures when query targets a region.

reasoning: Query asks for major sake breweries in Niigata. Tool fetches local specialties (food/craft GI items) and returns edamame, snow-buried carrots, and lotus root — all agricultural GI foods. No breweries and no sake-related results appear. The tool is wrong for this intent; there is no brewery data in the corpus.
missing_data: Sake brewery data: Niigata has 90+ breweries (Ponshukan, Asahi-Shuzo Kubota, Hakkaisan etc.). No brewery entity type exists in the MCP; GI food data does not cover breweries.
improvement_hint: Add a brewery/winery entity category sourced from official sake association registries or Niigata sake tourism data. Alternatively, tag relevant Wikidata brewery QIDs so get_spots can surface them.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts with hot springs in Hakuba. The response includes a municipal nav page about UN Tourism designation, a generic 'tourist office' entry, Hakuba Oike lake, an art museum, and a botanical garden — no ski resorts, no onsen facilities. The two key constraints (ski + onsen) are unmet.
missing_data: Hakuba ski resorts: Hakuba 47, Hakuba Goryu, Happo-One, Tsugaike Kogen; onsen: Mimizuku no Yu, Hakuba Onsen area. No lodging/resort type classification in get_spots.
improvement_hint: Tag municipal scrape entries with activity type (ski, onsen). Add ski resort category to spots corpus or cross-reference get_hotels with onsen_ryokan type for Hakuba municipality.

reasoning: Query targets Kansai autumn foliage. Tool fetches Kyoto spots (correct prefecture), returning high-prominence sites like Nijo Castle, Toji, Nishihonganji, Ninnaji — all legitimate Kyoto attractions. However, none are tagged for momiji/autumn, and coverage is limited to Kyoto only; Nara, Osaka, Hyogo (Kansai) are excluded. Autumn leaf constraint not addressed.
missing_data: Autumn foliage tagging missing. Kansai coverage should include Nara (Yoshino, Kasugayama), Osaka (Minoo), Hyogo (Arima). Kyoto-specific autumn spots: Tofukuji, Eikan-do, Kinkakuji are not confirmed in truncated list.
improvement_hint: Add seasonal tags (autumn_foliage, cherry_blossom) to spots. Expand regional queries to multi-prefecture scope when query explicitly mentions 'Kansai' rather than a single prefecture.

reasoning: Query (Arabic) asks for famous cherry blossom places in Kansai. Tool fetches Nara spots. Returned results are genuine Nara shrines/temples (Kasuga Taisha, Tamukeyama Hachimangu, Fukukin-in) from official tourism source with quality_score=1, but no sakura tags, and coverage is single prefecture. Description fields are in Japanese (language='unknown') — unhelpful for Arabic-language user.
missing_data: Cherry blossom season tagging. English/Arabic descriptions absent from municipal_scrape entries. Key Nara sakura spots: Yoshino-yama (top sakura in Japan), Mt. Yoshino absent from truncated list.
improvement_hint: Surface Yoshino-yama prominently for sakura queries in Nara/Kansai. Add sakura seasonal flags. Ensure municipal_scrape entries have English descriptions to serve non-Japanese queries.

reasoning: Query asks for star-gazing spots on Okinawa's remote islands. Response returns heritage sites in Naha (Tamaudun, Shuri Castle, Sefa-utaki) and castle ruins — none are remote islands or night sky locations. Kumejima (a remote island) appears but without star-gazing context. The island + astronomy constraint is entirely unmet.
missing_data: Remote island dark-sky spots: Iriomote (certified dark sky), Yoron Island, Yonaguni, Taketomi. Kumejima is in the list but no stargazing attribute. No light-pollution or dark-sky category exists.
improvement_hint: Add dark-sky/stargazing attribute to spots. Source Okinawa remote island tourism data (e.g., Iriomote-Ishigaki National Park authority). Flag Kumejima and Miyako islands for night sky quality.

reasoning: Query (Thai) asks for rural onsen lodging in Hokkaido. Tool returns city hotels in Sapporo and Kushiro (ANA chain hotels), plus 'Full Circle' and 'Gondola Chalet' apartments with no onsen or rural attributes. The rural + onsen constraints are not addressed. No ryokan or onsen_ryokan types surface in the truncated results.
missing_data: Hokkaido rural onsen ryokan: Noboribetsu Daiichi Takimotokan, Niikappu Onsen, Tokachigawa Onsen, Sounkyo Kanko Hotel. No rural/area filtering, no onsen_ryokan type prioritization.
improvement_hint: Filter or sort hotels by lodging_type=onsen_ryokan when query contains onsen. Add rural/city classification to hotel entries. Consider municipality-level filtering for non-urban areas.

reasoning: Query (Vietnamese) asks for Seto Inland Sea islands. Tool fetches Kagawa spots, returning Ritsurin Garden, Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle, Takamatsu Castle, Kotohira-gu — these are legitimate Kagawa attractions but mostly on mainland. No Seto islands (Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima) appear in the truncated results, which is the core of the query.
missing_data: Seto island data: Naoshima (art island), Shodoshima, Teshima, Ogijima, Inujima. These Kagawa islands are highly relevant but absent or buried. Island type attribute missing from spots.
improvement_hint: Tag spots with island=true for archipelago entries. Add Naoshima and Shodoshima as high-prominence Wikidata entries. Consider a dedicated island filter parameter for get_spots.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool returns 3 official METI craft items: Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (indigo-related textile), Otani Ware (ceramics — less relevant), and Awa Washi (indigo-dyed washi paper). The indigo weave and washi are relevant; Otani Ware is tangential. However, there are no workshop venues or workshop locations — only product designations.
missing_data: Actual workshop/experience venues: Aizome Experience Hall in Tokushima City, Yamashiro Shoji indigo studio. No craft experience/workshop location data; METI designations list products not visitor experiences.
improvement_hint: Supplement METI craft data with workshop/experience venue scraping from municipal tourism pages. Add an 'experience_available' flag or workshop URL to craft items when regional tourism sites list them.

reasoning: Query (German) asks for shukubo (temple lodging) on Mt. Koya. Tool returns Wakayama prefecture hotels: HOTEL KII TANABE, Myoji House guesthouse, WAKAYAMA House, an onsen ryokan in Kushimoto, Nanki-Shirahama Marriott — none are shukubo or located on Koyasan (municipality=高野町). The very specific constraint (shukubo + Koyasan) is completely unmet.
missing_data: Koyasan shukubo: Ekoin, Hongakuin, Rengejoin, Muryokoin, Henjoko-in. None appear in Wakayama hotel list. Koyasan municipality (Koya-cho) data appears absent or unranked.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo as a lodging_type category. Ensure Koya-cho municipal data is scraped and indexed. Consider adding temple-stay accommodation as a distinct entity type with religious institution cross-reference.

reasoning: Query asks for off-the-beaten-path samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 82 matches including Chiran Samurai Residences (Kagoshima), Aizu Bukeyashiki (Fukushima), Kanazawa Nagamachi district description, Shimane Buke Yashiki museum — geographically diverse, all outside Kyoto, well-grounded in real locations. Heritage designations (Important Preservation District) add credibility.
missing_data: Kakunodate samurai district (Akita) would strengthen the results. Some entries lack coordinates. Uchiko (Ehime) and Izushi (Hyogo) are also notable but coverage is acceptable.
improvement_hint: Ensure Kakunodate buke yashiki has a Wikidata entry indexed. Add coordinates to entries missing them (e.g. Fukui Uchiyama family residence). Relevance scoring would help surface highest-prominence non-Kyoto results first.

reasoning: Query (Indonesian) asks for summer festivals in Akita. Tool returns 3 festival items: Dainichido Bugaku (ritual dance — January, not summer), Kakunodate Festival (September), Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival (July — correct summer). No date for two items. Missing Akita's most famous summer festival (Akita Kanto Festival, August). Coverage is sparse (count=3) and no items explicitly match summer.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (August, UNESCO heritage) — the most famous Akita summer festival — is absent. Noshiro Neburi Nagashi, Omagari Fireworks. Only 3 items returned vs rich summer festival calendar.
wrong_data: Hayachine Kagura (national_heritage item) is from Iwate/Hanamaki, not Akita — geography is incorrect for this scope.
improvement_hint: Scrape Akita prefecture tourism event calendar for summer festivals. Add date ranges to festival items. Fix Hayachine Kagura scope attribution (Iwate, not Akita).

reasoning: Query (Korean) asks for small fishing port towns in Sanin region. Tool returns Shimane spots: Iwami Ginzan mine, Matsue Castle, Gassantoda Castle, Hamada Castle, Lake Shinji, Izumo Taisha — historically important but none are fishing port towns. The 'small harbor town' constraint is unmet; inland/historical sites dominate.
missing_data: Sanin fishing villages: Kotoura, Tottori Sakaiminato (crab port), Hamada fishing port area, Misumi, Masuda coastal area. No coastal/fishing village categorization in spot data.
improvement_hint: Add spot category tags (coastal, fishing_village, port_town) to wikidata and scrape entries. Source coastal tourism data from Shimane and Tottori municipal pages specifically for harbor/fishing communities.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper-making in Gifu. Tool returns 8 craft items: Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquer), Nagiso Rokuro Zaiku (woodwork), Ichii Ittobori (carving) — all legitimate Gifu crafts but only partially relevant. The key item — Mino Washi (美濃和紙, METI designated) — should appear but the truncation cuts before confirming it. No workshop/experience venue locations are provided.
missing_data: Mino Washi (美濃和紙) designation and experience venues: Warabi-za in Mino City, Udatsu no Machinami washi experience. These are the core answer to the query but may be in untruncated data.
improvement_hint: Confirm Mino Washi appears in full results (not truncated). Add washi experience venue links from Mino City tourism page. Prioritize craft items matching the query keyword in result ordering.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool returns Ishikawa spots dominated by Kanazawa attractions (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, National Crafts Museum, Oyama Shrine) — all in the city. Noto Peninsula fishing villages (Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho) are absent from the truncated list. The peninsula-specific constraint is unmet; results skew toward Kanazawa city.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula fishing villages: Wajima morning market, Sosogi coast, Noto-cho Ushitsu fishing port, Suzu Senmaida terraced rice fields near coast. Nanao Castle (Seven Ukai) is in the list but not a fishing village.
improvement_hint: Add municipality-level filtering for Noto Peninsula municipalities (Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho). Tag coastal/fishing village spots. Prominence scoring should not over-weight Kanazawa when query specifies Noto Peninsula.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area returns mostly Tottori-related results (municipal pages, GI product for Tottori rakkyō), shell middens in Hokkaido (not dunes), and a Katsushika park. Actual lesser-known dunes (Nakatajima, Sarufutsu, Chigasaki) are absent. The 'not Tottori' constraint is explicitly violated — Tottori content dominates.
missing_data: Lesser-known Japanese sand dunes: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Shizuoka, 2nd largest), Sarufutsu Dunes (Hokkaido), Hamasaka Dunes (Hyogo), Shin-Nishiwaki Dunes. None appear in results.
wrong_data: High砂貝塚 (Hokkaido midden/archaeological site) has no relation to sand dunes despite matching 砂 character.
improvement_hint: Add sand dune category to geomorphic/natural attractions. Ensure wikidata coverage includes Nakatajima Dunes (Q11303025) and Sarufutsu Dunes. Filter out character-match false positives (貝塚 matching 砂).

reasoning: Query (Chinese) asks for traditional crafts in Sanin region. Tool returns 4 Shimane METI-designated crafts in Chinese: Iwami-yaki ceramics, Sekishu Washi paper, Unshū soroban abacus, Izumo stone lanterns — all authentic Shimane specialties with full Chinese descriptions, production areas, and source URLs. Excellent coverage for a relatively small prefecture.
missing_data: Tottori (also Sanin) crafts not covered — tool only queries Shimane. Tottori: Inshū Washi, Tottori Mingei pottery. Query says 'Sanin region' which includes both prefectures.
improvement_hint: Support multi-prefecture regional queries (Sanin = Shimane + Tottori). Add a region parameter or allow comma-separated prefectures so 山陰 queries automatically fetch both Shimane and Tottori.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool returns general Hokkaido spots: Matsumae Castle, Goryokaku, Showa-shinzan, Sapporo Clock Tower, Asahi Bridge, Daisetsuzan — all legitimate Hokkaido attractions but none are lavender fields. No botanical/flower field category exists. The 'beyond Furano' qualifier and lavender constraint are entirely unmet.
missing_data: Hokkaido lavender fields beyond Furano: Nakafurano Flower Park, Farm Tomita (technically Furano but the benchmark), Shikisai-no-Oka (Biei), Kamifurano lavender fields. No flower farm category in spots data.
improvement_hint: Add flower farm / botanical field as a spot category. Tag Shikisai-no-Oka (Biei) and other Hokkaido flower farms in the corpus. Seasonal attribute would help filter for summer lavender relevance.

reasoning: Query (Filipino) asks for traditional old towns in Kyushu ('mga tradisyonal na bayan' = traditional towns; 'kape' likely means old/historic in Tagalog context, i.e., 古い). Tool returns Oita spots: Usa Jingu, Beppu Hells, Oka Castle, Beppu Tower, Usuki Stone Buddhas — legitimate Oita attractions but not specifically 'traditional old townscapes'. Kyushu-wide scope ignored; only Oita queried.
missing_data: Kyushu historic townscapes: Yanagawa canal district (Fukuoka), Chiran samurai town (Kagoshima), Yufuin historic area, Hita Mameda-machi old town (Oita). Usuki castle town is relevant but not highlighted.
improvement_hint: Support Kyushu regional queries across multiple prefectures. Tag spots with townscape/historic district classification. Add Chiku area preservation districts to improve old-town query coverage.

reasoning: Query (Japanese) asks for machiya/kominka (old folk house) stays in Hokuriku. Tool returns Ishikawa hotels: 'Student housing 1' (a hostel with no name), AB Hotel, ANA Crowne Plaza Kanazawa, 'かなや' guesthouse — no kominka or machiya lodging. The 古民家 (old folk house stay) constraint is completely unmet; urban chain hotels dominate.
missing_data: Hokuriku kominka stays: Gassho-zukuri farmhouses in Shirakawa-go (Gifu/Toyama border), Suzu-city kominka stays in Noto, various machiya guesthouses in Kanazawa. Shirakawa-go and Toyama data absent.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=kominka or machiya as a category. Source kominka stay data from Hokuriku tourism board. Include Gifu/Toyama (also Hokuriku) for gassho-zukuri farmhouse stays in Shirakawa-go and Gokayama.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. Tool returns Japan Heritage stories for Wakayama: Wakanoura scenic story and Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage. These are relevant cultural heritage narratives but not specifically 'mountain shrines'. Koyasan, Kumano Hongu Taisha, Nachi Taisha (the core mountain shrine complex) should appear — they may be in other stories in the full 7 items but the truncated sample shows coastal/pilgrimage content.
missing_data: Kumano Sanzan mountain shrines (Hongu, Nachi, Hayatama) and Koyasan explicitly as mountain worship sites. Story 074 covers pilgrimage but mountain shrine specifics need cross-referencing with Wikidata heritage items.
improvement_hint: Add keyword relevance scoring to Japan Heritage tool so mountain-shrine queries surface Kumano Sanzan story (if present) above coastal scenic stories. Cross-link japan_heritage items with relevant wikidata spots.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns 129 Niigata festival events. The Nagaoka Fireworks Festival (大花火大会, Aug 2-3) appears with detailed description — excellent. However, many non-fireworks events are returned (lavender festival, lily festival). Query specifies Sea of Japan coast, which implies other prefectures (Ishikawa, Toyama, Akita) too. Coverage of Niigata is solid but scope is limited.
missing_data: Other Sea of Japan coast fireworks: Nagahama Fireworks (Shiga/lake), Wajima Kiriko Festival fireworks, Akita Omagari Fireworks (nationally famous). Non-fireworks events dilute results.
improvement_hint: Add event_subtype=fireworks filter for festival queries containing 花火. Expand Sea of Japan coast queries to multiple prefectures. Filter or rank fireworks events higher when query specifically targets hanabi.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool returns 2 METI craft items: Hakata Ori (textile — multi-prefecture including Oita) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts (Beppu Takezaiku). The bamboo craft item is exactly on-target with excellent detail: bamboo types (Madake, Hachiku), 8 weaving techniques, production areas (Beppu, Oita, Hita). Hakata Ori is peripheral but valid for multi-prefecture coverage.
missing_data: Bamboo craft workshop venues in Beppu. Only 2 items total — small count but the core item (Beppu Bamboo Crafts) is highly specific and accurate.
improvement_hint: Link Beppu Bamboo Crafts to workshop experience venues (Beppu Takezaiku Center). Consider adding a workshops/experiences layer to complement METI designation data.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool returns Ehime wikidata spots: Uwajima Castle, Matsuyama Castle, Ozu Castle, Uchiko-za, Bansuiso — legitimate Ehime attractions but no cycling route data, no rural road/path information, and coverage is only Ehime (not all Shikoku). Cycling infrastructure constraint is completely unmet.
missing_data: Shikoku cycling routes: Shimanami Kaido (Ehime→Hiroshima, most famous), Tosa countryside cycling (Kochi), Tokushima Yoshino River cycling. No route/trail data type exists in MCP.
improvement_hint: Add cycling route/trail as a spot category. Source Shimanami Kaido and Shimanto River cycling data. Consider route entities with distance, difficulty, start/end points as structured fields.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. Tool returns 183 traditional arts items starting with Bunraku, Kabuki (UNESCO entry — correct hit), Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku. Kabuki appears as a national art form entry but without venue/location data for regional performances. Items lack geographic specificity needed to answer 'where outside Tokyo/Kyoto'. No filtering out Tokyo/Kyoto venues.
missing_data: Regional kabuki venues: Uchiko-za (Ehime, famous community kabuki), Takigi Kabuki at Konpira Shrine (Kagawa), Hitachiza (Ibaraki). Kabuki entry has no associated location or regional performance data.
improvement_hint: Cross-reference traditional arts entities with venue spots (theaters, shrines hosting performances). Add location/prefecture attributes to traditional art performance entries. Filter against query constraints (not Tokyo/Kyoto) in result ranking.
